mapping.a_element: tight-binding Hamiltonian of the 2D honeycomb lattice (graphene)
mapping.b_element: generalization of graphene's electronic structure to 4D fermion action

mapping.a_element: continuum limit yielding 2+1-dimensional quantum electrodynamics (QED) Hamiltonian
mapping.b_element: relativistic Dirac equation in 4D with exact chiral symmetry

mapping.a_element: symmetries of free Dirac fermions (spatial inversion, time reversal, charge conjugation, chirality)
mapping.b_element: exact chiral symmetry in 4D fermion action
requirement of two fermion species due to no-go theorems (implied symmetry constraints)

brief_justification: Both papers map lattice-based tight-binding models to continuum relativistic Dirac fermion theories, preserving key symmetries (e.g., chirality). Paper A derives 2+1D QED from graphene's honeycomb lattice, while Paper B generalizes this to 4D, showing the same underlying mechanism of fermionic field theory with symmetry constraints drives both.
